layers.name: Application Orchestration
layers.description: Entrypoints and module composition that bootstrap the app and wire subsystems.

layers.description: Core framework primitives (DI/event bus/logging), configuration, shared state/data, and reusable utilities.

layers.name: CNC/Geometry Domain Engine
layers.description: Router-bit, offset, boolean, and scheduling logic that models machining behavior.

layers.name: Editing & Interaction Services
layers.description: 2D editing workflows and interaction managers for canvas tools, selection, and panel manipulation.

layers.description: Three.js 3D visualization/CSG projection and CAD export adapters.

layers.name: UI & Delivery Surface
layers.description: UI-facing modules and external delivery surfaces such as styles and tests.

tour.title: Start at the real runtime entry (legacy orchestrator)
tour.focus: Understand where app state, managers, canvas wiring, and modular bridge are initialized.
tour.whyItMatters: This file is the operational spine that still coordinates 2D/3D behavior, bit lifecycle, and exports in production flow.
lookFor: Manager creation order and global references
Where CSG scheduling hooks are attached

tour.title: Then map the modular bootstrap and DI contracts
tour.focus: See how the container/event-driven module architecture is intended to compose subsystems.
tour.whyItMatters: This explains the migration path from monolithic script orchestration toward module-based composition.

tour.title: Read shared foundation: state, events, logging
tour.focus: Track how cross-module state changes and telemetry are emitted and observed.
tour.whyItMatters: Most higher-level behaviors (view mode, mesh repair telemetry, tool updates) become understandable once state + logging flow is clear.

tour.title: Understand the 2D drawing surface and viewport math
tour.focus: Learn how SVG viewport, zoom, pan, grid, and coordinate conversion behave.
tour.whyItMatters: Every editor interaction and panel/bit rendering update depends on this stable viewport foundation.
lookFor: How zoomUtils is reused by canvas logic

tour.title: Study profile editing orchestration (2D authoring workflow)
tour.focus: Trace how editor state, rendered segments, and active tools are synchronized.
tour.whyItMatters: This is the core 2D authoring engine that ultimately feeds toolpaths and 3D cutter generation.

tour.title: Connect editor geometry to CNC bit/panel domain models
tour.focus: See how user-defined profile geometry and bit parameters become operational bit instances tied to panel space.
tour.whyItMatters: This is where abstract editor geometry turns into machining semantics (operations, parameters, placement).
lookFor: Bit payload generation and variable collection
Panel anchor/size impact on bit coordinates

tour.title: Follow CNC operation and offset computation pipeline
tour.focus: Understand how bit moves trigger ordered recomputation of offsets/booleans and resulting part contours.
tour.whyItMatters: This stage is the geometric heart of the CNC workflow in 2D, feeding both previews and downstream 3D generation.
lookFor: Custom offset handling for path segments

tour.title: Understand when heavy 3D CSG actually runs
tour.focus: See debounce/serialization behavior between 2D edits and expensive 3D updates.
tour.whyItMatters: Prevents thrashing during interaction and ensures latest intent reaches the 3D pipeline.

tour.title: Enter the 3D subsystem orchestrator
tour.focus: Track 3D lifecycle, panel rebuild signatures, bit extrusion creation, and CSG delegation.
tour.whyItMatters: ThreeModule is the façade that joins scene/rendering concerns with machining geometry output.
lookFor: How extrusions are built per bit
Where CSG result visibility and selection targets are refreshed

tour.title: Dive into extrusion construction and boolean execution
tour.focus: Understand how 2D contours become cutter solids and how panel-minus-cutters CSG is executed/cached.
tour.whyItMatters: This is the key transformation from design intent to manufacturable 3D result.
lookFor: CSG signature cache and mesh replacement lifecycle

tour.title: Follow mesh validation/repair before output
tour.focus: Learn the repair gates that protect CSG/export workflows from invalid topology.
tour.whyItMatters: CNC/CAD outputs are only useful when mesh quality is robust; this layer enforces that.
lookFor: How export path consumes repair results

tour.title: Finish at CAD export (DXF/STL)
tour.focus: Read how geometric state is serialized into downstream manufacturing formats.
tour.whyItMatters: This is the delivery boundary where all upstream architecture converges into CNC-consumable artifacts.
lookFor: STL mesh collection and final file emission
